doom.driver: Iran oil facilities hit for first time as war with US-Israel enters day 9. Attacks spark huge fires as they hit four storage facilities and an oil production transfer centre in Tehran and Alborz.
doom.blurb: THEY JUST SET THE OIL ON FIRE
doom.summary: Nine days in and the U.S.-Israel campaign has crossed a significant threshold: Iranian oil infrastructure is now burning for the first time, with four storage facilities and a production centre in flames. Meanwhile Iran's Assembly of Experts has quietly named Khamenei's successor, suggesting Tehran is digging in rather than folding — despite Trump publicly telling them to 'cry uncle.' Add a U.S. embassy bombing in Oslo, Israeli special forces killing 41 in Lebanon, Bahrain's desalination plant hit, and Kuwait's oil production being cut — this war is metastasizing faster than anyone's containment doctrine.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: War enters day 9 with Iranian oil infrastructure struck for the first time, Gulf states reporting civilian infrastructure hits, and no signs of diplomatic off-ramp materializing.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Iran's Assembly of Experts announces a new Supreme Leader successor while Tehran vows no surrender, suggesting institutional continuity of the Islamic Republic despite military pressure.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Ars Technica reports apparent Iranian state hackers attempting to hijack consumer security cameras globally — low-grade cyber escalation on the margins.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: U.S. embassy in Oslo struck by explosion and a drone targeted RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus, signaling the conflict is now generating kinetic spillover into NATO-adjacent European soil.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: No major AI alignment developments today; the existential risk bandwidth is fully occupied by flesh-and-blood geopolitical catastrophe.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, weather-dominated scene, Tehran basin choked under a catastrophic layered atmosphere — the primary visual is a wall of thick black oil smoke billowing upward from multiple burning petroleum storage tanks simultaneously, the smoke so dense it becomes the sky itself, swallowing the upper frame entirely in near-black, while below the smoke ceiling the air is a hellish deep crimson haze, the city's chronic brown pollution smog fused with burning fuel particulate into a suffocating orange-red murk that filters every surface in sickly ember light — the snow-capped Alborz mountain peaks are just barely visible as pale grey silhouettes above the smoke line in the far background, ghostlike and indifferent, their white caps glowing faintly orange from the fires below — in the mid-ground, two enormous collapsed floating-roof petroleum storage tanks sit ruptured and caved inward, their cylindrical walls peeling outward like crushed tin cans, the fuel inside burning in deep churning pools of dark red fire — a suburban street in the foreground is flooded with crude oil running black along cracked asphalt gutters, pooling in potholes and reflecting the hellfire above in mirror-black orange, a knocked-over bicycle half-submerged at the road's edge, the oil lapping at it — tall silver refinery distillation columns rise through the haze behind the tanks, intact but surrounded by burning pipeline networks at their base, pipes ruptured and venting dark smoke — the entire scene color-graded in deep crimson, near-black, and suffocating amber, extreme contrast, no clean air anywhere in frame, no text, no antialiasing.

dawn.driver: First HBCU D1 women's wrestlers compete at championship. It's a weekend of firsts in Iowa, where the first national women's college wrestling championship is taking place and the first HBCU Division 1 women's wrestling team is fielding players.
dawn.blurb: HISTORY PINNED TO THE MAT IN IOWA
dawn.summary: In a week when global headlines have been consumed by war, missile strikes, and cascading crises, a quiet landmark was made in an Iowa arena: the first HBCU Division I women's wrestling team stepped onto the national championship mat, making history that will outlast the news cycle. This is also the inaugural national women's college wrestling championship itself — a sport finally given its full due. These athletes trained through chaos, competed through doubt, and showed up anyway. That's not a footnote. That's the signal.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Student athletes from an HBCU making D1 history at the first-ever national women's wrestling championship embodies exactly the kind of quiet, earned triumph that endures.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: China signaling 'thorough preparations' for a Trump-Xi meeting amid the Iran war suggests diplomatic back-channels remain alive even as front-channels burn.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Physicists discovering a new 'Island of Inversion' that breaks nuclear physics' long-held rules is a reminder that the universe still has surprises waiting for curious minds.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: NASA DART mission data revealing asteroids trade material like 'cosmic snowballs' deepens our understanding of planetary defense and the solar system's interconnected dynamics.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Nepal's landslide democratic election, led by a reform movement born from Gen Z protests, shows civic renewal is possible even in small, mountainous nations squeezed by giant neighbors.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Extreme close-up filling the entire frame with a worn wrestling shoe — flat-soled, ankle-hugging, laced tight — resting at the very edge of a regulation wrestling mat's boundary circle, the curved red mat edge cutting diagonally across the lower frame in warm amber and deep gold tones. The shoe's canvas fabric shows pixel-perfect creasing and wear, broken in from years of training. Soft green rubberized mat texture fills the upper portion of the frame, the center circle's edge just barely visible at the top. The shoe's sole presses deliberately against the threshold line — one foot claiming the edge of history. Reflected in the glossy mat surface below, a ghostly amber glow suggests overhead tournament lighting flooding down from above. At the very border of the frame, blurred in soft pixel bokeh, the hem of a singlet in deep royal blue is visible — an HBCU uniform, implied rather than shown. Morning-clean light washes the scene from the upper left, lifting shadows from the mat surface, casting the shoe in warm amber clarity. The color palette is amber, soft green, and gold against the neutral rubberized floor, with the emotional weight of a first step onto ground never walked before.
